title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
summary: The successful completion of Artemis 2 marks a significant milestone in NASA's space exploration program, but its future progress hinges on consistent funding. The mission's success has sparked discussions about the affordability and necessity of human spaceflight.

title: Voyager to fly private astronaut mission to ISS
summary: Voyager Technologies is set to become the third company to conduct a private astronaut mission to the International Space Station (ISS), leveraging the experience to advance its commercial space station plans.

title: Aethero orders Titan satellite to advance space-based data center ambitions
summary: Aethero is set to launch its most powerful computing payload, Titan, this fall to enable space-based data center capabilities and support large-scale AI workloads in orbit. The move aims to expand the company's ambitions in providing in-space processing solutions.

title: PlanetiQ secures $15 million Air Force STRATFI contract
summary: PlanetiQ secured a $15 million U.S. Air Force STRATFI contract to develop and launch satellites with advanced instruments for terrestrial and space weather data collection. This contract highlights the government's recognition of the company's technology.

title: Space Force reorg signals end of SDA as standalone agency
summary: The Space Force is reorganizing, ending the Space Development Agency (SDA) as a standalone entity. The SDA's 'go fast' model will continue under a new portfolio-based structure within the Space Force.

title: Seraphim forms space advisory council
summary: Seraphim Space, an early-stage space investor, has established a global advisory council to guide its long-term strategy amid rapid geopolitical and technological changes in the space sector.

title: Meink: Space Force must ‘execute’ as budget set to surge
summary: The U.S. Space Force must prioritize execution despite workforce shortages and acquisition delays, as its budget is set to significantly increase. Service leaders emphasize the need to overcome bottlenecks to deliver new technologies effectively.

title: Defining acquisition on a wartime footing
summary: The space industry is shifting to a wartime footing due to geopolitical changes, with key leaders emphasizing the urgency of rapid acquisition. This strategic shift reflects a response to evolving threats and the need for accelerated development.

title: NASA seeks proposals for commercial TDRSS replacement
summary: NASA is soliciting proposals for commercial communications services to replace its aging TDRSS relay satellites, aiming to transition from government-owned to commercially operated systems through Project NEXUS.

title: Emerson Launches Innovative Software-Driven Method for High-Fidelity Aerospace RF Testing
summary: Emerson introduced the NI Channel Emulator System Software (CHESS) platform at the 2026 Space Symposium, offering a software-defined method for high-fidelity aerospace RF testing. This innovation aims to enhance validation for mission-critical radio frequency systems in aerospace and defense.

title: How to see once-in-a-lifetime Comet PanSTARRS tonight before it disappears for good
summary: Comet C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS is approaching its perihelion, offering a rare viewing opportunity before it disappears. The comet's close pass of the sun will be visible tonight.

title: Max Space unveils new expandable space habitat for the moon and beyond: 'We need real estate that is scalable'
summary: Max Space has introduced a new expandable space habitat designed for lunar habitation and deep space missions, emphasizing scalability for long-duration flights.

title: A new Earthrise: An Apollo historian experiences Artemis 2
summary: An Apollo historian reflects on the anticipation and excitement surrounding the Artemis 2 Moon mission, comparing it to the historic Apollo missions.

title: SpaceX fires up next-gen 'Version 3' Starship ahead of landmark May test flight (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully completed the first full-duration static fire test of the upper stage of its next-gen 'Version 3' Starship, paving the way for a planned test flight next month.

title: How do supergiant exoplanets form? James Webb Space Telescope finds a clue
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to study the giant exoplanet 29 Cygni b, which may help clarify the distinction between planets and stars. This research could provide insights into the formation of supergiant exoplanets.

title: Space combat was 'critical to mission success' in US war in Iran, Space Force chief says
summary: The U.S. Space Force chief stated that space combat was crucial to mission success during the ongoing war in Iran, highlighting the service's combat credibility. This marks a significant shift in the perception of the Space Force's operational role.

title: The 7 best sci-fi movie soundtracks of all time
summary: The article lists the seven best sci-fi movie soundtracks of all time, highlighting classics like 'The Matrix' and 'Tron: Legacy'.

title: NASA astronauts joke around with an optical illusion on the ISS | Space photo of the day for April 15, 2026
summary: Two NASA astronauts playfully pretended to hold a robotic cargo spacecraft during its capture on the ISS on April 13, 2026. The moment was shared as part of a space photo of the day.

title: Fly higher and longer for less with $120 off this sub-9-ounce Potensic Atom 2 drone
summary: A drone sale offers 25% off the Potensic Atom 2, a sub-9-ounce drone with three batteries providing up to 90 minutes of flight time.

title: A dark energy tool just created the most comprehensive 3D map of our universe ever: 'This is a major paradigm shift'
summary: The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument has completed a five-year mission to create the most comprehensive 3D map of the universe ever. Its work is expected to lead to major scientific breakthroughs in understanding dark energy.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures on the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the crew's activities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Ship 39 Completes Static fire ahead of Booster 19 test Wednesday
summary: Ship 39 completed a static fire test in preparation for the upcoming Booster 19 test. The successful return of Orion has shifted focus within the Artemis program.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. The mission marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase's Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying four astronauts. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This occurs as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its midpoint.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The exact location for this achievement has been highlighted as optimal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved guidance systems. This innovation could enhance mission precision and efficiency in space operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that microgravity enhances propulsion efficiency, with potential applications for solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology. The chip's versatility could enable more efficient and compact systems for space applications.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS), marking a new competitor in the space industry. Axiom Space previously held a monopoly on NASA's private ISS missions, but this development introduces fresh players for 2026.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has announced the formation of a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies generating over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite data services. This follows a trend of increased space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the financial planning behind major projects.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's claims, while NASA challenges them to prove their assertions wrong. The dispute was highlighted during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform consolidates multiple functions into a single interface for streamlined operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit focused on working with the Pentagon and NASA, led by a former Texas representative. The robotic construction company ICON aims to expand its operations into government partnerships.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has unveiled a flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company is positioning the suit as a blend of functionality and style, reminiscent of vintage cargo pants.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace for orbital data centers to simplify securing orbital compute resources. This initiative aims to accelerate the growth of the orbital data center market.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Reductions in satellite launch prices and demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers are making orbital space data centers more appealing. The industry is exploring architecture options for these orbital facilities.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force is transitioning the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) to a new acquisition executive. This change is part of the Space Force's broader acquisition structure rollout.

title: COMSO Targets Expanding Working Capital Fund and Determining CASR Funding
summary: Space Systems Command’s Commercial Space Office (COMSO) is aiming to expand its Working Capital Fund and determine funding for the Commercial Assessment of Space Resiliency (CASR). The initiative focuses on leveraging commercial space capabilities.

title: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer Shares Enabling Tech for Artemis II
summary: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer discussed the technologies provided by Teledyne for the Artemis II mission in an interview with Via Satellite during the Space Symposium.

title: Moog Space SVP Mark Covelli Details Meteor Satellite Bus at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Space's senior vice president Mark Covelli discussed the company's support for the Artemis II mission and its investment in the Meteor satellite bus during the Space Symposium.

title: Lonestar Expands Launch Plans for Orbital Data Storage Payload
summary: Lonestar announced a launch date for StarVault, the world's first commercially operational space-based sovereign data storage platform. The company highlighted growing demand for off-planet data storage solutions.

title: US Space Command Chief Outlines Need for Maneuver Warfare Strategy
summary: U.S. Space Command Chief emphasizes the need for a space maneuver warfare strategy due to intensified Chinese activities in orbit, including refueling and logistics operations.

title: Integrated Satcom-Terrestrial Gateway Solution for Resilient ATC Management and Modernization
summary: A new integrated satcom-terrestrial gateway solution has been introduced to enhance resilient Air Traffic Control (ATC) management and support modernization efforts. The solution aims to improve communication reliability and efficiency in air traffic systems.

title: Comtech Achieves Major Milestone with Delivery of First EDIM Modems to U.S. Army
summary: Comtech has delivered the first EDIM modems to the U.S. Army, marking a significant achievement in military communications technology. This milestone highlights Comtech's role in advancing secure and reliable communication systems for defense applications.

title: Rocket Lab Closes Mynaric Acquisition at Higher Price Than Originally Agreed
summary: Rocket Lab completed its acquisition of Mynaric AG for $155.3 million, exceeding the original deal price by $5.3 million. The purchase focuses on Mynaric's optical communications terminal technology.
